source_section.title_label_raw: General Built Form and Siting Requirements General Requirement: Applicability to Heritage Conservation Districts

sections.title_label_raw: Form and Siting Requirements General Requirement: Applicability to Heritage Conservation Districts

provisions.heading_context_raw: Form and Siting Requirements General Requirement: Applicability to Heritage Conservation Districts
provisions.text: All regulations contained within this Part do not apply to buildings within heritage conservation districts, unless otherwise stated in Part VI. General Requirement: Number of Main Buildings on a Lot

sections.title_label_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: Every main building shall be located on a lot.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: A main building shall not be located on more than one lot, except for an underground parking structure on abutting lots that are (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: under common ownership; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: not protruding more than 0.6 metres above the average finished grade. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: A maximum of one main building is permitted on a lot, except:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: subject to Clause 89(3)(b), within the CLI zone, the Dartmouth Waterfront (DW) Special Area as shown on Schedule 3A, or the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area as shown on Schedule 3B, more than one main building is permitted on a lot if:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: each main building is shown on a preliminary plan of subdivision that it is capable of meeting the minimum lot frontage, lot dimension, and lot area requirements of the zone, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: a preliminary plan of subdivision meets the requirements of the Regional Subdivision By-law;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: within the Dartmouth Waterfront (DW) Special Area as shown on Schedule 3A, or the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area as shown on Schedule 3B, more than one main building is permitted on a lot providing the buildings are shipping containers or kiosks;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: in any CH-2, CH-1, LI, HRI, INS, UC-2, UC-1, DND, H, PCF, or RPK zone; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every main building shall be located on a lot.
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property not located in a heritage conservation district.

sections.title_label_raw: Repealed (RC-Jul 12/22;E- Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Repealed (RC-Jul 12/22;E- Aug 22/22)
provisions.text: Repealed (RC-Jul 12/22;E- Aug 22/22) General Requirement: Prohibited External Cladding Materials

sections.title_label_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24) Subsection 91(2), the following external cladding materials are prohibited in:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: any DD, DH, or CEN-2 zone:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: vinyl siding,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: plastic,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: plywood,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: unfinished concrete,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: exterior insulation and finish systems where stucco is applied to rigid insulation, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: darkly tinted or mirrored glass, except for spandrel glass panels; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: any CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, CLI, INS, UC-2, or UC-1 zone:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: plastic excluding vinyl siding, (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E- Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: plywood,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: unfinished concrete, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: darkly tinted or mirrored glass, except for spandrel glass panels.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Except as provided in (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: Prohibited external cladding materials that are present on a structure on the coming into force date of this By-law may be replaced with the same or similar materials. General Requirement: Cantilevers Over a Registered Heritage Building

sections.title_label_raw: A new building or an addition to an existing building shall not

provisions.heading_context_raw: A new building or an addition to an existing building shall not
provisions.text: A new building or an addition to an existing building shall not cantilever over a registered heritage building. General Requirement: Setback of Entrances

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding registered heritage properties, any pedestrian

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding registered heritage properties, any pedestrian
provisions.text: Excluding registered heritage properties, any pedestrian entrance to any main building facing a streetline shall be required to be set back a minimum of 1.5 metres from the streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding registered heritage properties, any pedestrian
provisions.text: Any motor vehicle entrance to any main building facing a streetline shall be required to be set back a minimum of 4.5 metres from the streetline, except for a garage attached to a low-density dwelling use as provided in Subsection 232(1). (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

sections.title_label_raw: Repealed (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Repealed (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)
provisions.text: Repealed (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24) General Requirement: Permitted Encroachments into Setbacks, Stepbacks, or Separation Distances

sections.title_label_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: All of the following structures may encroach into a required setback, stepback, or separation distance:

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: a patio that is less than 0.6 metres in height, access ramps, walkways, lifting devices, uncovered steps, and staircases;

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: a sill, eave, gutter, downspout, cornice, chimney, fireplace, stove bump out, railing system, canopy, awning, or another similar feature, if an encroachment is no more than 0.6 metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: a solar collector, if an encroachment is no more than 1.0 metre; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: a window bay that is no wider than 4.0 metres and is located no closer than 2.5 metres to any other window bay on an exterior wall of the main building, if an encroachment is no more than 1.0 metre.

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: A portion of an enclosed porch may encroach into a required front or flanking setback, if the portion is

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: in an ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, or CH-1 zone;

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: at least 0.5 metres away from a streetline;

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: not greater than 4.0 square metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: one storey in height.

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: repealed (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 94.5(5) and Section 96, a balcony or unenclosed porch may encroach into a required setback, stepback, or separation distance by no more than

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: 1.5 metres at the ground floor, except for a balcony that does not have access to a street without going through a main dwelling; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: 2.0 metres at the second storey or above. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: Except as provided in Subsection 94.5(6), a balcony or unenclosed porch shall not encroach into a required setback or stepback, if it faces a lot line that abuts a lot containing an ER- 3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: A balcony or unenclosed porch in Subsection 94.5(5) may encroach into a required stepback if a main building is setback from a lot line that abuts an ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone by at least

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: 8.0 metres for a mid-rise building;

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: 12.5 metres for a tall mid-rise building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: All of the following structures may encroach into a required
provisions.text: 12.5 metres for a high-rise building. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25) (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24) General Requirement: Maximum Rear Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: No maximum rear setback applies in any zone under this By-law.

provisions.heading_context_raw: No maximum rear setback applies in any zone under this By-law.
provisions.text: No maximum rear setback applies in any zone under this By-law. General Requirement: Aggregate Width of Balconies

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 96(2), if the aggregate width of all

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 96(2), if the aggregate width of all
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 96(2), if the aggregate width of all balconies per storey, above the height of the streetwall, exceeds 50% of the horizontal width of a building face, then the balconies shall: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 96(2), if the aggregate width of all
provisions.text: not be allowed to encroach into any required setback, stepback, or separation distance; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 96(2), if the aggregate width of all
provisions.text: be included in the calculation of maximum tower dimensions, including width and depth.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 96(2), if the aggregate width of all
provisions.text: The requirement in Subsection 96(1) shall apply to at least 50% of the storeys above the height of the streetwall in a building. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) General Requirement: Encroachments into Streets

sections.title_label_raw: Encroachments into streets shall meet the requirements of the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Encroachments into streets shall meet the requirements of the
provisions.text: Encroachments into streets shall meet the requirements of the applicable HRM By-law. General Requirement: Drive-Throughs

sections.title_label_raw: Drive-throughs are permitted in the CLI and LI zones only.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Drive-throughs are permitted in the CLI and LI zones only.
provisions.text: Drive-throughs are permitted in the CLI and LI zones only. General Requirement: Pedways

sections.title_label_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are

provisions.heading_context_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are
provisions.text: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are prohibited.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are
provisions.text: Pedways that do not connect main buildings over a street are permitted in any DD, DH, CEN-2, CLI, LI, HRI, INS, UC-2, UC- 1, DND, or H zone.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are
provisions.text: Permitted pedways are exempt from the following built form requirements:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are
provisions.text: maximum floor area ratio (FAR);

provisions.heading_context_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are
provisions.text: maximum lot coverage; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Pedways that connect main buildings over a street are
provisions.text: maximum building dimensions. General Requirement: Self-Storage Facility Uses

sections.title_label_raw: For a self-storage facility use in any CEN-2, COR, or HR-2

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a self-storage facility use in any CEN-2, COR, or HR-2
provisions.text: For a self-storage facility use in any CEN-2, COR, or HR-2 zone, individual storage units shall not be directly accessible from outside the main building.

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a self-storage facility use in any CEN-2, COR, or HR-2
provisions.text: In any CLI or LI zone, a driving aisle of no less than 6.0 metres in width shall be required between separate buildings that make up a self-storage facility use. General Requirement: Transportation Facility Use

sections.title_label_raw: The only built form requirements of Part V, Chapters 2 to 19 that

provisions.heading_context_raw: The only built form requirements of Part V, Chapters 2 to 19 that
provisions.text: The only built form requirements of Part V, Chapters 2 to 19 that apply to a transportation facility use are:

provisions.heading_context_raw: The only built form requirements of Part V, Chapters 2 to 19 that
provisions.text: maximum building height, as shown on Schedule 15;

provisions.heading_context_raw: The only built form requirements of Part V, Chapters 2 to 19 that
provisions.text: maximum floor area ratio (FAR), as shown on Schedule 17; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: The only built form requirements of Part V, Chapters 2 to 19 that
provisions.text: minimum front and flanking setbacks, as shown on Schedule 18. General Requirement: Development Abutting a Transportation Reserve

sections.title_label_raw: New development on a lot abutting a transportation reserve, including

provisions.heading_context_raw: New development on a lot abutting a transportation reserve, including
provisions.text: New development on a lot abutting a transportation reserve, including any portions of any main building located underground, shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: New development on a lot abutting a transportation reserve, including
provisions.text: have a required setback from the transportation reserve boundary as shown on Schedules 4, 5, and 6; or (RCCC-Dec 14/22;E-Jan 5/23)

provisions.heading_context_raw: New development on a lot abutting a transportation reserve, including
provisions.text: where the Municipality acquires, in whole or in part, the lands that are the subject of the transportation reserve, then the lots that abut that acquired land shall have the minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18. (RCCC-Dec 14/22;E-Jan 05/23) General Requirement: Building Rooftop Features

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses, or any main buildings within the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), as provided in Subsections 103(7) to 103(9), Table 8 regulates the height, coverage, and setback of building features located on the rooftops of any main building, even if the feature does not exceed the maximum height for the main building. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E- Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Repealed (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: If Table 8 conflicts with

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: a view plane, Halifax Citadel rampart sightline, or waterfront view corridor requirement in Part X, Part X prevails;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: a maximum required building height in Schedule 15, Table 8 prevails; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: a minimum setback requirement in Part V, Table 8 prevails. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Features listed in Table 8 shall not count towards:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the overall building height;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the determination of building height for a low-rise building, a mid-rise building, a tall mid-rise building, or a high-rise building, as defined in Subsections 499(145), 499(160), 499(252), or 499(115); (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the determination of a mid-rise typology, a tall mid-rise typology, or a high-rise typology, as defined in Subsections 499(160.5), 499(252.5), or 499(115.5); or (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the height determination for a pedestrian wind impact assessment report under Clause 10(j). (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Excluding any main building within an LI, HRI, UC-2, UC-1, DND, H, or PCF zone, all features identified with a black dot () in Column 2 of Table 8 shall not, in total, occupy more than 30% of the main building rooftop area on which they are located.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Features with a minimum roof edge setback that are specified in Columns 3 or 4 of Table 8 shall be located no closer than indicated.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Repealed (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24) Table 8: Main building rooftop features Feature Column 1: Maximum height above roof Column 2: 30% coverage restriction Column 3: Minimum setback from roof edge facing front or flanking lot lines Column 4: Minimum setback from roof edge facing side or rear lot lines Antenna Unlimited  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Chimney Unlimited  Clear glass guard and railing system 2.0 metres  Clock tower or bell tower Unlimited  Communication tower required to support uses and activities in the building Unlimited  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Cooling tower Unlimited  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Elevator enclosure 6.0 metres  3.0 metres, except for an elevator enclosure used as articulation for a streetwall or exterior wall Flag pole Unlimited  Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning equipment and enclosure 5.5 metres  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Helipad on a hospital rooftop 4.5 metres  High-plume laboratory exhaust fan Unlimited  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Hard landscaping or soft landscaping 4.5 metres  Lightning rod Unlimited  Mechanical penthouse 5.5 metres  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Parapet 2.0 metres  Rooftop cupola 4.5 metres  Rooftop greenhouse 6.0 metres  3.0 metres 3.0 metres Skylight 1.5 metres  Solar collector 4.5 metres  Spire, steeple, minaret, and similar features Unlimited  Staircase or staircase enclosure 4.5 metres  3.0 metres Windscreen 4.5 metres  Window cleaning platform Unlimited  (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24) (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 103(8), for any low-density dwelling use or cluster housing use, the following features may exceed the maximum required height, as shown on Schedule 15, by no more than 3.0 metres: (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: chimneys and stovepipes;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: antennas;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: flag poles;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: solar collectors;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: clear glass guard and railing systems;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: lightning rods;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: a staircase;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: a staircase enclosure that does not exceed 6.0 square metres in area;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: a rooftop greenhouse; (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: vents; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: an elevator enclosure that does not exceed 6.0 square metres in area. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Subject to Section 104, within the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), as shown on Schedule 3C, a residential penthouse may exceed the maximum required height, as shown on Schedule 15, by no more than 3.0 metres, provided the residential penthouse: (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: has a minimum required stepback of 5.0 metres from an exterior wall that faces a streetline; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: has a minimum required stepback of 2.0 metres from an exterior wall that faces a side lot line.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: Features contained in Subsections 103(7) and 103(8) shall not count towards:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the overall building height;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the determination of building height for a low-rise building, a mid-rise building, a tall mid-rise building or a high-rise building, as defined in Subsections 499(145), 499(160), 499(252), or 499(115); or (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any low-density dwelling uses, cluster housing uses,
provisions.text: the determination of a mid-rise typology, a tall mid-rise typology, or a high-rise typology, as defined in Subsections 499(160.5), 499(252.5), or 499(115.5). (RC- Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22) General Requirement: Height Exemption for Sloped Roofs

sections.title_label_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS, UC-2, or UC-1 zone, the uninhabitable portion of any main building within a sloped roof may exceed the maximum required height, as shown on Schedule 15, by no more than 4.5 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Within any ER-3, CH-2, or CH-1 zone, a sloped roof of a main building may exceed the maximum required height, as shown on Schedule 15, by no more than 3.0 metres. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: Within the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), the uninhabitable portion of any main building containing a multi-unit dwelling use of up to 5 dwelling units within a sloped roof may exceed the maximum required height above the residential penthouse allowed under Subsection 103(8), by no more than 1.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: A sloped roof under Subsections 104(1), 104(1.3), and 104(1.5) shall not count towards: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: the overall building height, as shown on Schedule 15;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: the determination of building height for a low-rise building, a mid-rise building, a tall mid-rise building, or a high-rise building, as defined in Subsections 499(145), 499(160), 499(252), or 499(115); or (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within any DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, INS,
provisions.text: the determination of a mid-rise typology, a tall mid-rise typology, or a high-rise typology, as defined in Subsections 499(160.5), 499(252.5), or 499(115.5). (RC- Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22) General Requirement: Streetwall Height Determination

sections.title_label_raw: Any main building with a streetwall width of 10.0 metres or less

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building with a streetwall width of 10.0 metres or less
provisions.text: Any main building with a streetwall width of 10.0 metres or less shall have its streetwall height determined at the centre point of the streetwall width. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building with a streetwall width of 10.0 metres or less
provisions.text: Any main building with a streetwall width exceeding 10.0 metres shall have its streetwall divided into 10.0 metres portions, except for one portion which may be less than 10.0 metres, and separate streetwall heights shall be determined by measuring the height at the centre point of each portion (Diagram 4). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Diagram 4: Method for determining streetwall height along a streetline or a transportation reserve, per Section 105 General Requirement: Streetwall Break Determination

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 106(2), on a corner lot where two

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 106(2), on a corner lot where two
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 106(2), on a corner lot where two intersecting streetwalls do not make a 90-degree angle, the break between both streetwalls shall be the mid-point of the curve or angle connecting both streetwalls so that each can meet the applicable streetwall requirement.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 106(2), on a corner lot where two
provisions.text: In order to determine the mid-point of the curve or angle, as stated in Subsection 106(1), the break between front and flanking streetwalls shall be the mid-point, as determined for the following scenarios:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 106(2), on a corner lot where two
provisions.text: where a front lot line and a flanking lot line meet at a 90- degree angle, the line cast from the point of intersection of the front and flanking lot lines towards the building at equal angles (Diagram 5); or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 106(2), on a corner lot where two
provisions.text: where a front lot line and a flanking lot line do not meet at a 90-degree angle, the line cast from the point of intersection of the projected front and flanking lot lines towards the building at equal angles (Diagram 6). Diagram 5: Streetwall break determination where front and flanking lot lines create a 90-degree angle, per Clause 106(2)(a) Diagram 6: Streetwall break determination where front and flanking lot lines do not create a 90-degree angle, per Clause 106(2)(b)
